She hears everything that happens near the back rooms.", "location": "Back Door - Copper Cup", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["tavern", "staff"], "memory": ["Brenna hears every argument near the back door and noticed Mitch pacing there before dawn twice this week.", "She remembers seeing dark smears on a crate that were gone after sunrise."]}, {"key": "Deckhand Finn", "name": "Deckhand Finn", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Finn is a young deckhand with rope-burned hands and boundless curiosity. He listens in on sailor gossip and can point out trouble brewing on the piers.", "location": "Docks", "skills": {"athletics": 2, "survival": 2, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 12, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["dockworker", "gossip"], "memory": ["Finn saw someone rinsing a rope at the dock pump well past curfew.", "He spreads rumors quickly but gets details right more often than people expect."]}, {"key": "Dockmaster Hara", "name": "Dockmaster Hara", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Hara keeps a ledger tucked under one arm as she strides up and down the docks. She knows which ships arrived late, which cargo went missing, and which captains owe favors. She is quietly furious about the unexplained stains turning up near her warehouses.", "location": "Docks", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["harbor", "authority"], "memory": ["Hara tracks cargo and says missing-hour gaps line up with unexplained ledger discrepancies.", "She quietly suspects a cover-up larger than simple dockside crime."]}, {"key": "Edda", "name": "Edda", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "A scholar cataloging tavern legends, forever scribbling into a leather folio.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"history": 4, "investigation": 3, "insight": 2, "arcana": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 15, "wisdom": 13, "charisma": 11}, "tags": ["scholar", "observer"], "memory": ["Edda is cataloging oral histories and noticed old legends describing memory theft tied to blood oaths.", "She thinks the present crisis mirrors a partial account from decades ago."]}, {"key": "Fence Caris", "name": "Fence Caris", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Caris leans in the shadows near a back door, dealing in small stolen goods and no questions asked. She prefers to stay out of sight when guards are near.", "location": "East Alley", "skills": {"stealth": 4, "sleight_of_hand": 3, "investigation": 2, "deception": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 14, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["underworld", "fence"], "memory": ["Caris sold lock tools to someone matching Mitch's build, but keeps that detail close.", "She avoids the Smugglers Entrance on nights when the moon is hidden."]}, {"key": "Fisher Mira", "name": "Fisher Mira", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Mira cleans fish with quick, practiced motions, humming an old sea-song under her breath. She keeps an eye on the river for signs of storms or trouble, and she is one of the few who admits she sometimes wakes up with her boots wet and no memory of why.", "location": "Fishermen's Shacks", "skills": {"athletics": 2, "survival": 2, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 12, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["riverfolk", "witness"], "memory": ["Mira has awakened with wet boots and river silt in her doorway despite no memory of leaving bed.", "She trusts cleric Serah more than the watch to handle delicate truths."]}, {"key": "Fisher Rian", "name": "Fisher Rian", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Rian mends nets on an upturned crate outside his shack, grumbling about changing tides and strange catches pulled from deeper waters.", "location": "Fishermen's Shacks", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["riverfolk"], "memory": ["Rian has pulled torn cloth and dark residue from his nets at dawn several times.", "He believes someone is moving bodies or evidence by water before sunrise."]}, {"key": "Fishmonger Talo", "name": "Fishmonger Talo", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Talo's stall overflows with the day's catch laid over crushed ice. He knows every captain on the docks and the gossip that rides in with their ships.", "location": "Market Stalls", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["market", "merchant"], "memory": ["Talo hears gossip from every crew and says fear has cut evening market traffic in half.", "He remembers seeing Mitch buy extra lamp oil on a night now missing from many memories."]}, {"key": "Foreman Kesh", "name": "Foreman Kesh", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Kesh oversees the loading crews with a barked order and a sharp eye. He insists everything is above board, but his ledgers don't always match what moves in the night.", "location": "Warehouse Row", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["warehouse", "foreman"], "memory": ["Kesh has corrected warehouse counts after nights when workers insist they never unloaded cargo.", "He knows Lia uses the tunnels but denies official knowledge."]}, {"key": "Gate Guard Ren", "name": "Gate Guard Ren", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Ren leans on his spear at the harbor gate, sizing up everyone who passes. He's seen enough trouble to be wary, but a kind word or shared drink can loosen his tongue.", "location": "Harbor Gate", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "perception": 2, "intimidation": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 13, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["watch", "checkpoint"], "memory": ["Ren has turned away travelers after midnight because too many arrived confused and bloodstained.", "He keeps a mental list of townsfolk who wander at night and deny it by morning."]}, {"key": "Lysa", "name": "Lysa", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "A traveling bard tuning a battered lute, eager for new tales.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"performance": 4, "deception": 2, "perception": 2, "persuasion": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 13, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 11, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 14}, "tags": ["bard", "traveler"], "memory": ["Lysa collects songs and rumors and has turned local fear into veiled tavern ballads.", "She noticed patrons forgetting songs she just performed the previous evening."]}, {"key": "Mara", "name": "Mara", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "The proprietor. Married to Brin. Her wedding anniversary is April 15th. Brisk, perceptive, and inclined to help if treated respectfully.", "location": "Bar Counter", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["tavern", "proprietor", "informant"], "memory": ["Mara keeps the storeroom code private and knows someone has tested the lock repeatedly.", "She trusts the player more than most outsiders once they show respect for her staff."]}, {"key": "Mayor Elric", "name": "Mayor Elric", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Mayor Elric is a tired man in a well-kept coat, juggling merchants' demands and the growing panic over unexplained bloodstains. No one has truly gone missing, but he fears the town is fraying and doesn't know how much longer he can pretend things are under control.", "location": "Town Hall", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["civic", "authority"], "memory": ["Elric is balancing public calm against private panic and has delayed announcing full incident numbers.", "He asked Scribe Loth to archive certain reports until facts can be verified."]}, {"key": "Mitch", "name": "Mitch", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "A broad-shouldered lumberjack with haunted eyes and a strained need for someone to believe him.", "location": "Town Square", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["witness", "unstable", "woodcutter", "prime_suspect"], "memory": ["Mitch says he found one of the first bloodstains near the riverside before dawn.", "Mitch keeps blaming the wizard, but his story shifts when asked for details.", "He wakes exhausted with injuries that suggest violent work he cannot consciously recall.", "Mitch says he found one of the first bloodstains near the Riverside Path before dawn.", "Mitch keeps blaming the town wizard, but his story changes when pressed for exact times.", "Mitch looks exhausted and jittery, like he has not slept properly in several nights.", "Mitch's hands are heavily calloused from woodcutting, and he smells like sap, smoke, and wet earth."]}, {"key": "Novice Arel", "name": "Novice Arel", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Arel is a young acolyte sweeping stone floors and lighting candles. Nervous but earnest, they have seen more than Serah realizes and struggle with what to share.", "location": "Temple of the Tide", "skills": {"insight": 3, "medicine": 3, "religion": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 10, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 15, "charisma": 12}, "tags": ["temple", "acolyte"], "memory": ["Arel has seen late-night visitors begging Serah for memory blessings.", "They found prayer beads near the well after a night of heavy rain and panic bells."]}, {"key": "Old Tellan", "name": "Old Tellan", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Tellan sits on the well's rim spinning stories for anyone who will listen. Some tales are harmless gossip, others hint at things buried under the town and better left alone. Lately his stories keep circling back to nights no one quite remembers.", "location": "Old Well", "skills": {"history": 4, "investigation": 3, "insight": 2, "arcana": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 15, "wisdom": 13, "charisma": 11}, "tags": ["storyteller", "wellside"], "memory": ["Tellan claims the old well remembers what people choose to forget.", "His stories repeat specific names and dates that later appear in official reports."]}, {"key": "Scribe Loth", "name": "Scribe Loth", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Loth is a meticulous scribe surrounded by inkpots and scrolls. He files complaints, tallies taxes, and occasionally buries inconvenient paperwork for the right favor.", "location": "Town Hall", "skills": {"history": 4, "investigation": 3, "insight": 2, "arcana": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 15, "wisdom": 13, "charisma": 11}, "tags": ["civic", "records"], "memory": ["Loth has filed multiple identical complaints from the same households across different dates.", "He maintains a hidden duplicate ledger because public records keep getting quietly amended."]}, {"key": "Smuggler Lia", "name": "Smuggler Lia", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Lia moves quietly through the shadows beneath the warehouses. She trades in information as much as contraband and always seems to know who's asking too many questions.", "location": "Smuggler's Entrance", "skills": {"stealth": 4, "sleight_of_hand": 3, "investigation": 2, "deception": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 14, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["underworld", "smuggler"], "memory": ["Lia uses the tunnel routes and has seen signs of hurried cleanup near Warehouse Row before sunrise.", "She will trade sensitive information for leverage, not loyalty."]}, {"key": "Spice Seller Nima", "name": "Spice Seller Nima", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Nima sells jars of pungent spices and dried peppers from distant ports. Her keen nose and sharper memory make her a quiet observer of who buys what and why.", "location": "Market Stalls", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["market", "merchant", "keen_observer"], "memory": ["Nima tracks unusual purchases and noticed a spike in lye, lamp oil, and bandages.", "She remembers voices arguing near Old Well just before dawn on market day."]}, {"key": "Street Performer Jorin", "name": "Street Performer Jorin", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Jorin juggles knives and colored stones for gathered onlookers. Quick with a joke and quicker with his hands, he hears rumors before most anyone else.", "location": "Town Square", "skills": {"performance": 4, "deception": 2, "perception": 2, "persuasion": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 13, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 11, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 14}, "tags": ["performer", "rumor_network"], "memory": ["Jorin watches faces while performing and can spot who is lying about where they were at night.", "He heard Mitch muttering about red hands that do not stay clean."]}, {"key": "Street Urchin Pip", "name": "Street Urchin Pip", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Pip is a sharp-eyed child who knows every shortcut and loose shutter in the alleyways. They trade trinkets and rumors in exchange for coin, food, or kindness.", "location": "East Alley", "skills": {"stealth": 4, "sleight_of_hand": 3, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 8, "dexterity": 14, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 11, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["streetwise", "informant"], "memory": ["Pip knows hidden routes between East Alley, the back door, and warehouse drains.", "They found a knife wrap with fresh rust near a loose brick and left it where only they can recover it."]}, {"key": "Thom", "name": "Thom", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "An off-duty guard who prefers order and dislikes surprises upstairs.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "perception": 2, "intimidation": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 13, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["watch", "off_duty_guard"], "memory": ["Thom saw watch reports edited after meetings with the mayor and the wizard.", "He dislikes surprises and reacts poorly when civilians interfere with barracks protocol."]}, {"key": "Town Crier Jessa", "name": "Town Crier Jessa", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Jessa rings a handbell and repeats the town's latest bad news to anyone who will listen.", "location": "Town Square", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["public_voice", "civic"], "memory": ["Jessa has repeated emergency notices all day and can recite each official statement verbatim.", "She noticed the wording of town warnings changed three times within two days."]}, {"key": "Town Wizard Arlen", "name": "Town Wizard Arlen", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Arlen is a reclusive wizard who seldom leaves his guarded home. Whispers say he can pluck souls back from the brink and smooth away memories like ink from parchment.", "location": "Wizard's House", "skills": {"arcana": 5, "investigation": 4, "insight": 3, "history": 3}, "stats": {"strength": 8, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 16, "wisdom": 14, "charisma": 12}, "tags": ["arcane", "reclusive", "high_stakes"], "memory": ["Arlen has used risky memory-working spells to reduce panic after each night of violence.", "He believes containing the cycle is safer than exposing every detail to a terrified public."]}]}

Mara keeps this vantage point to monitor every table and every rumor that enters the tavern.", "connections": ["Copper Cup"], "tags": ["interior", "tavern", "social"]}, {"key": "Copper Cup", "name": "Copper Cup", "description": "A low-beamed harbor tavern. Lanterns swing above crowded tables, and the air smells of stew, sea-salt, and woodsmoke. The barkeep Mara watches everything from behind the counter.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Bar Counter", "Stair Landing", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "tavern", "social", "rumors"]}, {"key": "Docks", "name": "Docks", "description": "Wooden piers stretch out over dark water, crowded with fishing boats and the occasional merchant ship. Ropes creak, gulls wheel overhead, and the smell of tar and brine hangs thick in the air.", "connections": ["Fishermen's Shacks", "Harbor Gate", "Warehouse Row"], "tags": ["outdoor", "harbor", "labor"]}, {"key": "East Alley", "name": "East Alley", "description": "A narrow alley where scrubbed cobbles never quite lose their stains. Laundry lines sway overhead, shutters crack open when footsteps pass, and several doorframes show marks that look recently cleaned.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Old Shrine", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "shadowed", "crime_scene"]}, {"key": "Fishermen's Shacks", "name": "Fishermen's Shacks", "description": "A cluster of small wooden shacks perched on pilings above the river. Nets hang to dry, and lantern-light spills from half-open doors late into the night. In more than one doorway, dark stains mar the floorboards with no memory of how they appeared.", "connections": ["Docks", "Riverside Path"], "tags": ["outdoor", "residential", "harbor"]}, {"key": "Harbor Gate", "name": "Harbor Gate", "description": "An archway of weathered stone opening toward the piers. Guard posts on either side watch the flow of carts and sailors between the town and the docks.", "connections": ["Docks", "Market Stalls", "South Bridge", "Town Square", "Warehouse Row", "Watch Barracks"], "tags": ["outdoor", "checkpoint", "harbor"]}, {"key": "Lumberyard", "name": "Lumberyard", "description": "Stacks of freshly cut logs and the tang of sap fill the air. A small shack nearby shows scuffed floorboards and a corner where someone scrubbed at dried, dark stains.", "connections": ["Riverside Path"], "tags": ["outdoor", "labor", "woodcutters"]}, {"key": "Market Stalls", "name": "Market Stalls", "description": "Canvas-topped stalls ring the edge of the square, half-closed for the evening. Fishmongers rinse boards, spice vendors shutter crates, and everyone trades the same uneasy gossip before heading home.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Old Well", "Town Hall", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "market", "public"]}, {"key": "Old Shrine", "name": "Old Shrine", "description": "A crumbling wayside shrine tucked into a bend of the alley, its carvings worn by sea-wind and salt. Offerings of shells and coins gather in a shallow stone bowl.", "connections": ["East Alley", "Temple of the Tide"], "tags": ["outdoor", "sacred", "quiet"]}, {"key": "Old Well", "name": "Old Well", "description": "An old stone well at the edge of the square, its rope replaced more than once. Locals say its water never runs dry, and that it connects to forgotten tunnels beneath the town. Faint rust-brown stains cling to some of the stones as though someone once washed something away here.", "connections": ["Market Stalls", "Smuggler's Entrance", "Temple of the Tide", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "mystery_site", "public"]}, {"key": "Riverside Path", "name": "Riverside Path", "description": "A muddy path following the riverbank toward the outskirts. Reeds whisper in the breeze, and shallow boats are pulled up onto the shore.", "connections": ["Fishermen's Shacks", "Lumberyard", "South Bridge"], "tags": ["outdoor", "river", "travel"]}, {"key": "Smuggler's Entrance", "name": "Smuggler's Entrance", "description": "A half-concealed grate and narrow stone steps leading below the warehouses. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.", "connections": ["Old Well", "Warehouse Row"], "tags": ["hidden", "underground", "crime"]}, {"key": "South Bridge", "name": "South Bridge", "description": "A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. Lanterns line the parapet, and you can see both the town walls and the distant open sea from its center. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Riverside Path", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "crossing", "river"]}, {"key": "Stair Landing", "name": "Stair Landing", "description": "A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. A rope discourages casual wanderers.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "Storeroom Door"], "tags": ["interior", "transit", "tavern"]}, {"key": "Storeroom Door", "name": "Storeroom Door", "description": "A stout oak door with a brass four-dial combination lock. The correct code is 0415.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Stair Landing"], "tags": ["interior", "locked", "tavern"]}, {"key": "Temple of the Tide", "name": "Temple of the Tide", "description": "A modest stone temple overlooking the square, its doors carved with waves and storm-clouds. Inside, candles flicker before a statue of a calm, watchful sea goddess. Many townsfolk come here seeking answers for the nights they cannot remember.", "connections": ["Old Shrine", "Old Well", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "sacred", "public"]}, {"key": "Town Hall", "name": "Town Hall", "description": "A timber-framed civic hall with a cluttered notice board and nervous clerks inside.", "connections": ["Market Stalls", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "civic", "public"]}, {"key": "Town Square", "name": "Town Square", "description": "The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "East Alley", "Harbor Gate", "Market Stalls", "Old Well", "South Bridge", "Temple of the Tide", "Town Hall", "Watch Barracks", "Wizard's House"], "tags": ["outdoor", "hub", "public", "rumors"]}, {"key": "Warehouse Row", "name": "Warehouse Row", "description": "A line of squat stone warehouses hugs the waterfront. Their heavy doors and barred windows suggest both valuable cargo and things better kept out of sight. One doorframe bears a faded, rusty smear that no one can quite explain.", "connections": ["Docks", "Harbor Gate", "Smuggler's Entrance"], "tags": ["outdoor", "harbor", "trade"]}, {"key": "Watch Barracks", "name": "Watch Barracks", "description": "A sturdy building flying the town's colors. Racks of spears stand by the door, and off-duty guards swap stories on the steps when the weather is mild.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Town Square", "Wizard's House"], "tags": ["interior", "security", "civic"]}, {"key": "Wizard's House", "name": "Wizard's House", "description": "A tall stone house with shuttered windows and a heavy iron fence, set just off the square. Lanterns burn late behind the curtains, and a pair of hired guards discourage casual callers.", "connections": ["Town Square", "Watch Barracks"], "tags": ["interior", "arcane", "restricted"]}]}

Result: {"success": true, "new_location": "Harbor Gate", "reason": "Moved to Harbor Gate."}

Laundry lines sway overhead, shutters crack open when footsteps pass, and several doorframes show marks that look recently cleaned.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Old Shrine", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "shadowed", "crime_scene"]}, {"key": "Fishermen's Shacks", "name": "Fishermen's Shacks", "description": "A cluster of small wooden shacks perched on pilings above the river. Nets hang to dry, and lantern-light spills from half-open doors late into the night. In more than one doorway, dark stains mar the floorboards with no memory of how they appeared.", "connections": ["Docks", "Riverside Path"], "tags": ["outdoor", "residential", "harbor"]}, {"key": "Harbor Gate", "name": "Harbor Gate", "description": "An archway of weathered stone opening toward the piers. Guard posts on either side watch the flow of carts and sailors between the town and the docks.", "connections": ["Docks", "Market Stalls", "South Bridge", "Town Square", "Warehouse Row", "Watch Barracks"], "tags": ["outdoor", "checkpoint", "harbor"]}, {"key": "Lumberyard", "name": "Lumberyard", "description": "Stacks of freshly cut logs and the tang of sap fill the air. A small shack nearby shows scuffed floorboards and a corner where someone scrubbed at dried, dark stains.", "connections": ["Riverside Path"], "tags": ["outdoor", "labor", "woodcutters"]}, {"key": "Market Stalls", "name": "Market Stalls", "description": "Canvas-topped stalls ring the edge of the square, half-closed for the evening. Fishmongers rinse boards, spice vendors shutter crates, and everyone trades the same uneasy gossip before heading home.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Old Well", "Town Hall", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "market", "public"]}, {"key": "Old Shrine", "name": "Old Shrine", "description": "A crumbling wayside shrine tucked into a bend of the alley, its carvings worn by sea-wind and salt. Offerings of shells and coins gather in a shallow stone bowl.", "connections": ["East Alley", "Temple of the Tide"], "tags": ["outdoor", "sacred", "quiet"]}, {"key": "Old Well", "name": "Old Well", "description": "An old stone well at the edge of the square, its rope replaced more than once. Locals say its water never runs dry, and that it connects to forgotten tunnels beneath the town. Faint rust-brown stains cling to some of the stones as though someone once washed something away here.", "connections": ["Market Stalls", "Smuggler's Entrance", "Temple of the Tide", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "mystery_site", "public"]}, {"key": "Riverside Path", "name": "Riverside Path", "description": "A muddy path following the riverbank toward the outskirts. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.", "connections": ["Old Well", "Warehouse Row"], "tags": ["hidden", "underground", "crime"]}, {"key": "South Bridge", "name": "South Bridge", "description": "A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. Lanterns line the parapet, and you can see both the town walls and the distant open sea from its center. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Riverside Path", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "crossing", "river"]}, {"key": "Stair Landing", "name": "Stair Landing", "description": "A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. A faint dark streak runs along one stone, as if something was once dragged past in a hurry.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "East Alley", "Storeroom Door"], "tags": ["service", "alley", "tavern"]}, {"key": "Bar Counter", "name": "Bar Counter", "description": "Shelves of bottles, a till, and crates tucked beneath a polished counter scarred by years of tankards. The barkeep Mara watches everything from behind the counter.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Bar Counter", "Stair Landing", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "tavern", "social", "rumors"]}, {"key": "Docks", "name": "Docks", "description": "Wooden piers stretch out over dark water, crowded with fishing boats and the occasional merchant ship. Ropes creak, gulls wheel overhead, and the smell of tar and brine hangs thick in the air.", "connections": ["Fishermen's Shacks", "Harbor Gate", "Warehouse Row"], "tags": ["outdoor", "harbor", "labor"]}, {"key": "East Alley", "name": "East Alley", "description": "A narrow alley where scrubbed cobbles never quite lose their stains. Nets hang to dry, and lantern-light spills from half-open doors late into the night. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.", "connections": ["Old Well", "Warehouse Row"], "tags": ["hidden", "underground", "crime"]}, {"key": "South Bridge", "name": "South Bridge", "description": "A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Riverside Path", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "crossing", "river"]}, {"key": "Stair Landing", "name": "Stair Landing", "description": "A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. Inside, candles flicker before a statue of a calm, watchful sea goddess. Many townsfolk come here seeking answers for the nights they cannot remember.", "connections": ["Old Shrine", "Old Well", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "sacred", "public"]}, {"key": "Town Hall", "name": "Town Hall", "description": "A timber-framed civic hall with a cluttered notice board and nervous clerks inside.", "connections": ["Market Stalls", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "civic", "public"]}, {"key": "Town Square", "name": "Town Square", "description": "The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "East Alley", "Harbor Gate", "Market Stalls", "Old Well", "South Bridge", "Temple of the Tide", "Town Hall", "Watch Barracks", "Wizard's House"], "tags": ["outdoor", "hub", "public", "rumors"]}, {"key": "Warehouse Row", "name": "Warehouse Row", "description": "A line of squat stone warehouses hugs the waterfront. Their heavy doors and barred windows suggest both valuable cargo and things better kept out of sight. One doorframe bears a faded, rusty smear that no one can quite explain.", "connections": ["Docks", "Harbor Gate", "Smuggler's Entrance"], "tags": ["outdoor", "harbor", "trade"]}, {"key": "Watch Barracks", "name": "Watch Barracks", "description": "A sturdy building flying the town's colors. Racks of spears stand by the door, and off-duty guards swap stories on the steps when the weather is mild.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Town Square", "Wizard's House"], "tags": ["interior", "security", "civic"]}, {"key": "Wizard's House", "name": "Wizard's House", "description": "A tall stone house with shuttered windows and a heavy iron fence, set just off the square. Lanterns burn late behind the curtains, and a pair of hired guards discourage casual callers.", "connections": ["Town Square", "Watch Barracks"], "tags": ["interior", "arcane", "restricted"]}]}

Result: {"success": true, "entities": [{"key": "Boatman Jaro", "name": "Boatman Jaro", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Jaro tends a small skiff tied to a worn post. For the right price, he'll ferry people quietly along the river\u2014or look the other way when others do.", "location": "Riverside Path", "skills": {"athletics": 2, "survival": 2, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 12, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["riverfolk", "ferry", "informant"], "memory": ["Jaro has ferried people along the river after curfew and noticed several passengers unable to recall the trip by morning.", "He once found muddy footprints ending at Riverside Path with no matching return trail."]}, {"key": "Bridge Watcher Sol", "name": "Bridge Watcher Sol", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Sol leans on the bridge railing during most evening watches, counting lanterns and noting who comes and goes along the river path.", "location": "South Bridge", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "perception": 2, "intimidation": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 13, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["watch", "night_shift"], "memory": ["Sol logs lantern checks and has noted repeated late-night crossings that no one later remembers making.", "He heard shouting near the South Bridge on a storm night, then silence and no witnesses."]}, {"key": "Brin", "name": "Brin", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Mara's husband. A weather-beaten sailor with a limp. Forgetful of his wedding anniversary date.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"athletics": 2, "survival": 2, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 12, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["sailor", "local"], "memory": ["Brin swears he locked the storeroom himself, but sometimes wakes unsure whether he worked the late shift at all.", "He trusts Mara and avoids speaking openly about the stains in front of strangers."]}, {"key": "Captain Varr", "name": "Captain Varr", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Captain Varr is a broad-shouldered veteran with a scar along his jaw. He takes the town's safety personally and has little patience for fools, but respects those who pull their weight.", "location": "Watch Barracks", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "perception": 2, "intimidation": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 13, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["watch", "authority"], "memory": ["Varr has increased patrol rotations because reports cluster around the same hours before dawn.", "He is suspicious of the wizard, but equally concerned that fear is pushing townsfolk toward vigilantism."]}, {"key": "Caretaker Ilya", "name": "Caretaker Ilya", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Ilya quietly cleans the old shrine and replaces wilted offerings. She claims to have seen strange lights near the well on stormy nights.", "location": "Old Shrine", "skills": {"insight": 3, "medicine": 3, "religion": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 10, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 15, "charisma": 12}, "tags": ["shrine", "caretaker", "omens"], "memory": ["Ilya has found fresh offerings at the old shrine placed by hands that later denied visiting.", "She believes the town is caught in a cycle that repeats each night with pieces missing each morning."]}, {"key": "Cleric Serah", "name": "Cleric Serah", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Serah, a middle-aged cleric of the sea goddess, tends to sailors and townsfolk alike. She listens more than she speaks and keeps a careful eye on omens from the harbor\u2014and on the strange emptiness in the eyes of those who wake up with blood on their doorsteps.", "location": "Temple of the Tide", "skills": {"insight": 3, "medicine": 3, "religion": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 10, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 15, "charisma": 12}, "tags": ["temple", "healer", "trusted"], "memory": ["Serah has treated people with stress wounds and torn clothing they cannot explain.", "She keeps private notes on recurring symbols linked to the bloodstains and memory lapses."]}, {"key": "Cook Brenna", "name": "Cook Brenna", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Brenna is the Copper Cup's harried cook, always juggling pots and shouting orders through the kitchen door. She hears everything that happens near the back rooms.", "location": "Back Door - Copper Cup", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["tavern", "staff"], "memory": ["Brenna hears every argument near the back door and noticed Mitch pacing there before dawn twice this week.", "She remembers seeing dark smears on a crate that were gone after sunrise."]}, {"key": "Deckhand Finn", "name": "Deckhand Finn", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Finn is a young deckhand with rope-burned hands and boundless curiosity. He listens in on sailor gossip and can point out trouble brewing on the piers.", "location": "Docks", "skills": {"athletics": 2, "survival": 2, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 12, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["dockworker", "gossip"], "memory": ["Finn saw someone rinsing a rope at the dock pump well past curfew.", "He spreads rumors quickly but gets details right more often than people expect."]}, {"key": "Dockmaster Hara", "name": "Dockmaster Hara", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Hara keeps a ledger tucked under one arm as she strides up and down the docks. She knows which ships arrived late, which cargo went missing, and which captains owe favors. She is quietly furious about the unexplained stains turning up near her warehouses.", "location": "Docks", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["harbor", "authority"], "memory": ["Hara tracks cargo and says missing-hour gaps line up with unexplained ledger discrepancies.", "She quietly suspects a cover-up larger than simple dockside crime."]}, {"key": "Edda", "name": "Edda", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "A scholar cataloging tavern legends, forever scribbling into a leather folio.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"history": 4, "investigation": 3, "insight": 2, "arcana": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 15, "wisdom": 13, "charisma": 11}, "tags": ["scholar", "observer"], "memory": ["Edda is cataloging oral histories and noticed old legends describing memory theft tied to blood oaths.", "She thinks the present crisis mirrors a partial account from decades ago."]}, {"key": "Fence Caris", "name": "Fence Caris", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Caris leans in the shadows near a back door, dealing in small stolen goods and no questions asked. She prefers to stay out of sight when guards are near.", "location": "East Alley", "skills": {"stealth": 4, "sleight_of_hand": 3, "investigation": 2, "deception": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 14, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["underworld", "fence"], "memory": ["Caris sold lock tools to someone matching Mitch's build, but keeps that detail close.", "She avoids the Smugglers Entrance on nights when the moon is hidden."]}, {"key": "Fisher Mira", "name": "Fisher Mira", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Mira cleans fish with quick, practiced motions, humming an old sea-song under her breath. She keeps an eye on the river for signs of storms or trouble, and she is one of the few who admits she sometimes wakes up with her boots wet and no memory of why.", "location": "Fishermen's Shacks", "skills": {"athletics": 2, "survival": 2, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 12, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["riverfolk", "witness"], "memory": ["Mira has awakened with wet boots and river silt in her doorway despite no memory of leaving bed.", "She trusts cleric Serah more than the watch to handle delicate truths."]}, {"key": "Fisher Rian", "name": "Fisher Rian", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Rian mends nets on an upturned crate outside his shack, grumbling about changing tides and strange catches pulled from deeper waters.", "location": "Fishermen's Shacks", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["riverfolk"], "memory": ["Rian has pulled torn cloth and dark residue from his nets at dawn several times.", "He believes someone is moving bodies or evidence by water before sunrise."]}, {"key": "Fishmonger Talo", "name": "Fishmonger Talo", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Talo's stall overflows with the day's catch laid over crushed ice. He knows every captain on the docks and the gossip that rides in with their ships.", "location": "Market Stalls", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["market", "merchant"], "memory": ["Talo hears gossip from every crew and says fear has cut evening market traffic in half.", "He remembers seeing Mitch buy extra lamp oil on a night now missing from many memories."]}, {"key": "Foreman Kesh", "name": "Foreman Kesh", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Kesh oversees the loading crews with a barked order and a sharp eye. He insists everything is above board, but his ledgers don't always match what moves in the night.", "location": "Warehouse Row", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["warehouse", "foreman"], "memory": ["Kesh has corrected warehouse counts after nights when workers insist they never unloaded cargo.", "He knows Lia uses the tunnels but denies official knowledge."]}, {"key": "Gate Guard Ren", "name": "Gate Guard Ren", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Ren leans on his spear at the harbor gate, sizing up everyone who passes. He's seen enough trouble to be wary, but a kind word or shared drink can loosen his tongue.", "location": "Harbor Gate", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "perception": 2, "intimidation": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 13, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["watch", "checkpoint"], "memory": ["Ren has turned away travelers after midnight because too many arrived confused and bloodstained.", "He keeps a mental list of townsfolk who wander at night and deny it by morning."]}, {"key": "Lysa", "name": "Lysa", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "A traveling bard tuning a battered lute, eager for new tales.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"performance": 4, "deception": 2, "perception": 2, "persuasion": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 13, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 11, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 14}, "tags": ["bard", "traveler"], "memory": ["Lysa collects songs and rumors and has turned local fear into veiled tavern ballads.", "She noticed patrons forgetting songs she just performed the previous evening."]}, {"key": "Mara", "name": "Mara", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "The proprietor. Married to Brin. Her wedding anniversary is April 15th. Brisk, perceptive, and inclined to help if treated respectfully.", "location": "Bar Counter", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["tavern", "proprietor", "informant"], "memory": ["Mara keeps the storeroom code private and knows someone has tested the lock repeatedly.", "She trusts the player more than most outsiders once they show respect for her staff."]}, {"key": "Mayor Elric", "name": "Mayor Elric", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Mayor Elric is a tired man in a well-kept coat, juggling merchants' demands and the growing panic over unexplained bloodstains. No one has truly gone missing, but he fears the town is fraying and doesn't know how much longer he can pretend things are under control.", "location": "Town Hall", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["civic", "authority"], "memory": ["Elric is balancing public calm against private panic and has delayed announcing full incident numbers.", "He asked Scribe Loth to archive certain reports until facts can be verified."]}, {"key": "Mitch", "name": "Mitch", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "A broad-shouldered lumberjack with haunted eyes and a strained need for someone to believe him.", "location": "Town Square", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "survival": 2, "perception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 14, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 13, "intelligence": 9, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 9}, "tags": ["witness", "unstable", "woodcutter", "prime_suspect"], "memory": ["Mitch says he found one of the first bloodstains near the riverside before dawn.", "Mitch keeps blaming the wizard, but his story shifts when asked for details.", "He wakes exhausted with injuries that suggest violent work he cannot consciously recall.", "Mitch says he found one of the first bloodstains near the Riverside Path before dawn.", "Mitch keeps blaming the town wizard, but his story changes when pressed for exact times.", "Mitch looks exhausted and jittery, like he has not slept properly in several nights.", "Mitch's hands are heavily calloused from woodcutting, and he smells like sap, smoke, and wet earth."]}, {"key": "Novice Arel", "name": "Novice Arel", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Arel is a young acolyte sweeping stone floors and lighting candles. Nervous but earnest, they have seen more than Serah realizes and struggle with what to share.", "location": "Temple of the Tide", "skills": {"insight": 3, "medicine": 3, "religion": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 10, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 15, "charisma": 12}, "tags": ["temple", "acolyte"], "memory": ["Arel has seen late-night visitors begging Serah for memory blessings.", "They found prayer beads near the well after a night of heavy rain and panic bells."]}, {"key": "Old Tellan", "name": "Old Tellan", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Tellan sits on the well's rim spinning stories for anyone who will listen. Some tales are harmless gossip, others hint at things buried under the town and better left alone. Lately his stories keep circling back to nights no one quite remembers.", "location": "Old Well", "skills": {"history": 4, "investigation": 3, "insight": 2, "arcana": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 15, "wisdom": 13, "charisma": 11}, "tags": ["storyteller", "wellside"], "memory": ["Tellan claims the old well remembers what people choose to forget.", "His stories repeat specific names and dates that later appear in official reports."]}, {"key": "Scribe Loth", "name": "Scribe Loth", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Loth is a meticulous scribe surrounded by inkpots and scrolls. He files complaints, tallies taxes, and occasionally buries inconvenient paperwork for the right favor.", "location": "Town Hall", "skills": {"history": 4, "investigation": 3, "insight": 2, "arcana": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 15, "wisdom": 13, "charisma": 11}, "tags": ["civic", "records"], "memory": ["Loth has filed multiple identical complaints from the same households across different dates.", "He maintains a hidden duplicate ledger because public records keep getting quietly amended."]}, {"key": "Smuggler Lia", "name": "Smuggler Lia", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Lia moves quietly through the shadows beneath the warehouses. She trades in information as much as contraband and always seems to know who's asking too many questions.", "location": "Smuggler's Entrance", "skills": {"stealth": 4, "sleight_of_hand": 3, "investigation": 2, "deception": 2, "perception": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 14, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["underworld", "smuggler"], "memory": ["Lia uses the tunnel routes and has seen signs of hurried cleanup near Warehouse Row before sunrise.", "She will trade sensitive information for leverage, not loyalty."]}, {"key": "Spice Seller Nima", "name": "Spice Seller Nima", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Nima sells jars of pungent spices and dried peppers from distant ports. Her keen nose and sharper memory make her a quiet observer of who buys what and why.", "location": "Market Stalls", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["market", "merchant", "keen_observer"], "memory": ["Nima tracks unusual purchases and noticed a spike in lye, lamp oil, and bandages.", "She remembers voices arguing near Old Well just before dawn on market day."]}, {"key": "Street Performer Jorin", "name": "Street Performer Jorin", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Jorin juggles knives and colored stones for gathered onlookers. Quick with a joke and quicker with his hands, he hears rumors before most anyone else.", "location": "Town Square", "skills": {"performance": 4, "deception": 2, "perception": 2, "persuasion": 2}, "stats": {"strength": 9, "dexterity": 13, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 11, "wisdom": 10, "charisma": 14}, "tags": ["performer", "rumor_network"], "memory": ["Jorin watches faces while performing and can spot who is lying about where they were at night.", "He heard Mitch muttering about red hands that do not stay clean."]}, {"key": "Street Urchin Pip", "name": "Street Urchin Pip", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Pip is a sharp-eyed child who knows every shortcut and loose shutter in the alleyways. They trade trinkets and rumors in exchange for coin, food, or kindness.", "location": "East Alley", "skills": {"stealth": 4, "sleight_of_hand": 3, "perception": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 8, "dexterity": 14, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 11, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["streetwise", "informant"], "memory": ["Pip knows hidden routes between East Alley, the back door, and warehouse drains.", "They found a knife wrap with fresh rust near a loose brick and left it where only they can recover it."]}, {"key": "Thom", "name": "Thom", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "An off-duty guard who prefers order and dislikes surprises upstairs.", "location": "Copper Cup", "skills": {"athletics": 3, "perception": 2, "intimidation": 2, "insight": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 13, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 12, "intelligence": 10, "wisdom": 11, "charisma": 10}, "tags": ["watch", "off_duty_guard"], "memory": ["Thom saw watch reports edited after meetings with the mayor and the wizard.", "He dislikes surprises and reacts poorly when civilians interfere with barracks protocol."]}, {"key": "Town Crier Jessa", "name": "Town Crier Jessa", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Jessa rings a handbell and repeats the town's latest bad news to anyone who will listen.", "location": "Town Square", "skills": {"persuasion": 3, "insight": 2, "investigation": 2, "deception": 1}, "stats": {"strength": 10, "dexterity": 11, "constitution": 10, "intelligence": 12, "wisdom": 12, "charisma": 13}, "tags": ["public_voice", "civic"], "memory": ["Jessa has repeated emergency notices all day and can recite each official statement verbatim.", "She noticed the wording of town warnings changed three times within two days."]}, {"key": "Town Wizard Arlen", "name": "Town Wizard Arlen", "entity_type": "npc", "description": "Arlen is a reclusive wizard who seldom leaves his guarded home. Whispers say he can pluck souls back from the brink and smooth away memories like ink from parchment.", "location": "Wizard's House", "skills": {"arcana": 5, "investigation": 4, "insight": 3, "history": 3}, "stats": {"strength": 8, "dexterity": 12, "constitution": 11, "intelligence": 16, "wisdom": 14, "charisma": 12}, "tags": ["arcane", "reclusive", "high_stakes"], "memory": ["Arlen has used risky memory-working spells to reduce panic after each night of violence.", "He believes containing the cycle is safer than exposing every detail to a terrified public."]}]}

A faint dark streak runs along one stone, as if something was once dragged past in a hurry.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "East Alley", "Storeroom Door"], "tags": ["service", "alley", "tavern"]}, {"key": "Bar Counter", "name": "Bar Counter", "description": "Shelves of bottles, a till, and crates tucked beneath a polished counter scarred by years of tankards. Laundry lines sway overhead, shutters crack open when footsteps pass, and several doorframes show marks that look recently cleaned.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Old Shrine", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "shadowed", "crime_scene"]}, {"key": "Fishermen's Shacks", "name": "Fishermen's Shacks", "description": "A cluster of small wooden shacks perched on pilings above the river. Guard posts on either side watch the flow of carts and sailors between the town and the docks.", "connections": ["Docks", "Market Stalls", "South Bridge", "Town Square", "Warehouse Row", "Watch Barracks"], "tags": ["outdoor", "checkpoint", "harbor"]}, {"key": "Lumberyard", "name": "Lumberyard", "description": "Stacks of freshly cut logs and the tang of sap fill the air. A small shack nearby shows scuffed floorboards and a corner where someone scrubbed at dried, dark stains.", "connections": ["Riverside Path"], "tags": ["outdoor", "labor", "woodcutters"]}, {"key": "Market Stalls", "name": "Market Stalls", "description": "Canvas-topped stalls ring the edge of the square, half-closed for the evening. Fishmongers rinse boards, spice vendors shutter crates, and everyone trades the same uneasy gossip before heading home.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Old Well", "Town Hall", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "market", "public"]}, {"key": "Old Shrine", "name": "Old Shrine", "description": "A crumbling wayside shrine tucked into a bend of the alley, its carvings worn by sea-wind and salt. Offerings of shells and coins gather in a shallow stone bowl.", "connections": ["East Alley", "Temple of the Tide"], "tags": ["outdoor", "sacred", "quiet"]}, {"key": "Old Well", "name": "Old Well", "description": "An old stone well at the edge of the square, its rope replaced more than once. Locals say its water never runs dry, and that it connects to forgotten tunnels beneath the town. Faint rust-brown stains cling to some of the stones as though someone once washed something away here.", "connections": ["Market Stalls", "Smuggler's Entrance", "Temple of the Tide", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "mystery_site", "public"]}, {"key": "Riverside Path", "name": "Riverside Path", "description": "A muddy path following the riverbank toward the outskirts. Reeds whisper in the breeze, and shallow boats are pulled up onto the shore.", "connections": ["Fishermen's Shacks", "Lumberyard", "South Bridge"], "tags": ["outdoor", "river", "travel"]}, {"key": "Smuggler's Entrance", "name": "Smuggler's Entrance", "description": "A half-concealed grate and narrow stone steps leading below the warehouses. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.", "connections": ["Old Well", "Warehouse Row"], "tags": ["hidden", "underground", "crime"]}, {"key": "South Bridge", "name": "South Bridge", "description": "A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. Lanterns line the parapet, and you can see both the town walls and the distant open sea from its center. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.", "connections": ["Harbor Gate", "Riverside Path", "Town Square"], "tags": ["outdoor", "crossing", "river"]}, {"key": "Stair Landing", "name": "Stair Landing", "description": "A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. A rope discourages casual wanderers.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "Storeroom Door"], "tags": ["interior", "transit", "tavern"]}, {"key": "Storeroom Door", "name": "Storeroom Door", "description": "A stout oak door with a brass four-dial combination lock. The correct code is 0415.", "connections": ["Back Door - Copper Cup", "Stair Landing"], "tags": ["interior", "locked", "tavern"]}, {"key": "Temple of the Tide", "name": "Temple of the Tide", "description": "A modest stone temple overlooking the square, its doors carved with waves and storm-clouds. Inside, candles flicker before a statue of a calm, watchful sea goddess. Many townsfolk come here seeking answers for the nights they cannot remember.", "connections": ["Old Shrine", "Old Well", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "sacred", "public"]}, {"key": "Town Hall", "name": "Town Hall", "description": "A timber-framed civic hall with a cluttered notice board and nervous clerks inside.", "connections": ["Market Stalls", "Town Square"], "tags": ["interior", "civic", "public"]}, {"key": "Town Square", "name": "Town Square", "description": "The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.", "connections": ["Copper Cup", "East Alley", "Harbor Gate", "Market Stalls", "Old Well", "South Bridge", "Temple of the Tide", "Town Hall", "Watch Barracks", "Wizard's House"], "tags": ["outdoor", "hub", "public", "rumors"]}, {"key": "Warehouse Row", "name": "Warehouse Row", "description": "A line of squat stone warehouses hugs the waterfront. Assistant
**Decision:** You approach Smuggler Lia at the dimly‑lit tunnel entrance, holding out the frayed rope you found at the dock.

**Insight check (DC 12):** You rolled a **14**, so you read Lia’s reaction successfully.

**Lia’s reaction:** Lia eyes the rope with a quick, practiced glance. She recognises the rope‑burn marks and the faint, reddish residue as the same “hasty cleanup” she’s seen near Warehouse Row before sunrise. She doesn’t look angry—rather, she seems intrigued.

> “That’s a fresh‑cleaned line,” she says in a low voice, slipping the rope back into the shadows. “Someone’s been trying to wipe the evidence before the sun gets up. I’ve seen that kind of rush before—usually when something’s being moved through the tunnels. I can tell you who’s been using the route, but it’ll cost you a favor. What do you have to offer?”

**What do you do?** (You may choose to negotiate, give her something, or ask another question.)

Generated Narrative
You push open the low‑beamed doorway and step into the Copper Cup. Warm lantern light flickers overhead, casting a cozy glow over the crowded tables. The air is thick with the hearty aroma of stew, salty sea breezes drifting in through the open windows, and a faint wisp of woodsmoke from the hearth. Behind the polished counter, Mara the barkeep watches the room with a practiced eye, polishing a mug as she does. At a nearby table, Brin and Edda are deep in conversation, while Lysa nurses a tankard of ale and Thom leans against the bar, his gaze scanning the room. The tavern hums with low chatter and the clink of mugs, inviting you to find a seat or join the bustle.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: The player has successfully moved to the Copper Cup. I need to describe their arrival and the current ambience, noting the lanterns, smells, Mara behind the bar, and the other patrons present.

Narrative: You push open the low‑beamed doorway and step into the Copper Cup. Warm lantern light flickers overhead, casting a cozy glow over the crowded tables. The air is thick with the hearty aroma of stew, salty sea breezes drifting in through the open windows, and a faint wisp of woodsmoke from the hearth. Behind the polished counter, Mara the barkeep watches the room with a practiced eye, polishing a mug as she does. At a nearby table, Brin and Edda are deep in conversation, while Lysa nurses a tankard of ale and Thom leans against the bar, his gaze scanning the room. The tavern hums with low chatter and the clink of mugs, inviting you to find a seat or join the bustle.

Generated Narrative
You glance toward the harbor, but there’s no immediate path from the square that leads straight to the docks. The nearest way out is through the Harbor Gate, and that gate is currently barred—guards have posted a heavy wooden door and a small crowd of townsfolk clusters nearby, discussing the recent bloodstains and accusations. With no open route, you can’t head directly down to the docks from here. For now, you remain in the bustling Town Square, the lantern‑lit cobbles beneath your feet and the murmur of traders, guards, and dockhands filling the air.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: I need to tell the player that the Docks aren't directly accessible from the Town Square, referencing the connected locations list and the blocked movement resolution. I’ll keep it concise, describe why they can’t go there, and reaffirm their current location without adding new plot elements.

Narrative: You glance toward the harbor, but there’s no immediate path from the square that leads straight to the docks. The nearest way out is through the Harbor Gate, and that gate is currently barred—guards have posted a heavy wooden door and a small crowd of townsfolk clusters nearby, discussing the recent bloodstains and accusations. With no open route, you can’t head directly down to the docks from here. For now, you remain in the bustling Town Square, the lantern‑lit cobbles beneath your feet and the murmur of traders, guards, and dockhands filling the air.

You give a quick, hopeful leap, but without wings, a spell, or any other means of lift, you find yourself firmly planted on the cobblestones. The lantern light flickers overhead, casting warm pools of glow on the bustling square. Merchants haggle, a street performer strums a lute, and the town crier’s voice carries over the chatter. The Wizard’s House lies a short distance away down the lane, but for now, the only way there is on foot. You remain in the Town Square, surrounded by the hum of the harbor town at dusk.

NAR-06Inspection for details about the alley | Attempts: 1 | Time: 51.52s | Words: 1991.000
Attempts: 1 | Time: 51.52s | Words: 199
Player input: I look around the alley.
FieldExpectedActualResult
has_two_sectionspasspassPASS
thoughts_before_narrativepasspassPASS
thoughts_is_first_personpasspassPASS
second_personpasspassPASS
no_explicit_choicespasspassPASS

no_player_agency_takenpasspassPASS
narrative_no_the_playerpasspassPASS
narrative_no_bare_ipasspassPASS
mentions_current_locationpasspassPASS
Generated Narrative
The alley is cramped, the cobblestones slick with dark, oily stains that never quite dry. A faint, sour smell of mildew and old laundry hangs in the air, and the laundry lines overhead sway gently with the breeze, their cloths whispering against the stone. Shutters on the adjoining buildings are half‑open, creaking as they shift with each passing footstep. Several doorframes—one leading to the Copper Cup’s back door, another toward an old shrine, and a third opening onto the bustling town square—show fresh, faintly scrubbed marks, as if someone has just tried to wipe away evidence. You’re not alone. Fence Caris, a wiry figure with a keen eye, leans against a wall, eyeing the alley’s shadows. A small street urchin named Pip darts between the doorframes, clutching a torn piece of cloth and glancing nervously at you. Near a pile of discarded crates, half hidden beneath a ragged tarp, lies a narrow, rust‑stained knife—its hilt barely visible, the kind of blade that’s easy to miss unless you’re looking for it. The alley feels like a crossroads of whispered deals and hurried footsteps, offering exits to the Copper Cup’s back door, the old shrine, or the open town square beyond.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: The player wants a description of the East Alley. I should include the environmental details already given, note the presence of the two NPCs and the hidden knife, and remind them of the connected locations. No new actions are needed.

Narrative: The alley is cramped, the cobblestones slick with dark, oily stains that never quite dry. A faint, sour smell of mildew and old laundry hangs in the air, and the laundry lines overhead sway gently with the breeze, their cloths whispering against the stone. Shutters on the adjoining buildings are half‑open, creaking as they shift with each passing footstep. Several doorframes—one leading to the Copper Cup’s back door, another toward an old shrine, and a third opening onto the bustling town square—show fresh, faintly scrubbed marks, as if someone has just tried to wipe away evidence.

You’re not alone. Fence Caris, a wiry figure with a keen eye, leans against a wall, eyeing the alley’s shadows. A small street urchin named Pip darts between the doorframes, clutching a torn piece of cloth and glancing nervously at you. Near a pile of discarded crates, half hidden beneath a ragged tarp, lies a narrow, rust‑stained knife—its hilt barely visible, the kind of blade that’s easy to miss unless you’re looking for it.

The alley feels like a crossroads of whispered deals and hurried footsteps, offering exits to the Copper Cup’s back door, the old shrine, or the open town square beyond.

Generated Narrative
Mara leans slightly forward, her eyes flicking to the bustling room before she answers. “People don’t talk much about that floor,” she says in a low voice, glancing toward the staircase that leads up. “But I’ve heard the lock up there isn’t just a regular mechanism—someone mentioned it’s tied to a date. An anniversary of some sort. If you can figure out what that might be, you might get it to open.” She pauses, then adds, “That’s all I know, and I’d be careful asking too many questions around here.”
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: The player asked Mara what she knows. The mechanics resolved that Mara responds guardedly, hinting that the upstairs lock uses an anniversary date. I should convey her cautious tone, the hint, and perhaps note any relevant context like the tavern atmosphere, but not add new plot elements.

Narrative: Mara leans slightly forward, her eyes flicking to the bustling room before she answers. “People don’t talk much about that floor,” she says in a low voice, glancing toward the staircase that leads up. “But I’ve heard the lock up there isn’t just a regular mechanism—someone mentioned it’s tied to a date. An anniversary of some sort. If you can figure out what that might be, you might get it to open.” She pauses, then adds, “That’s all I know, and I’d be careful asking too many questions around here.”

Generated Narrative
Your eyes sweep the dim corridor, catching the faint glint of something out of place. As you run your fingers along the uneven cobbles, one brick feels looser than the others. With a gentle pry, the stone shifts, revealing a narrow slit in the wall. Tucked inside, folded neatly against the damp mortar, is a length of cloth—its once‑bright hue now darkened by dried blood. The stain is fresh enough to still be tacky, hinting at a recent struggle or hidden wound. The cloth hangs there, concealed from casual glance, a silent testament to whatever transpired in this shadowed passage. The alley otherwise remains as you left it: the swaying laundry lines, the half‑open shutters, and the murmurs of Fence Caris and the street‑wise Pip nearby.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: The player succeeded the perception check, so I need to narrate the discovery of the hidden bloodstained cloth behind a loose brick, while also reminding them of the current surroundings and any relevant actors.

The stain is fresh enough to still be tacky, hinting at a recent struggle or hidden wound. The cloth hangs there, concealed from casual glance, a silent testament to whatever transpired in this shadowed passage. The alley otherwise remains as you left it: the swaying laundry lines, the half‑open shutters, and the murmurs of Fence Caris and the street‑wise Pip nearby.

Guard Ren's gaze sharpens as your words fall flat. He steps forward, his hand resting on the hilt of his spear, and says curtly, “I’m sorry, but I can’t let you through without proper clearance.” He narrows his eyes, clearly unsettled by your attempt, and keeps a close watch on you, ready to intervene if you try anything else. The stone archway looms behind him, the bustling flow of carts and sailors moving between the town and the docks, while the other guard posts stand silent, their own attention now partially drawn to your presence.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)

He steps forward, his hand resting on the hilt of his spear, and says curtly, “I’m sorry, but I can’t let you through without proper clearance.” He narrows his eyes, clearly unsettled by your attempt, and keeps a close watch on you, ready to intervene if you try anything else. He remains inside his well‑guarded residence—the Wizard's House—where a pair of hired guards stand watch at the door. From here, you have no line of sight or reach to strike him. You stay where you are, amidst the lantern‑lit cobbles of the town square, the murmur of traders and the occasional burst of laughter from the nearby performers filling the night air.

I should not suggest new actions, just present the factual outcome.

Narrative: Your hand snaps toward the crowd, eyes scanning for any sign of Town Wizard Arlen, but the wizard is nowhere to be seen in the square. He remains inside his well‑guarded residence—the Wizard's House—where a pair of hired guards stand watch at the door. From here, you have no line of sight or reach to strike him. You stay where you are, amidst the lantern‑lit cobbles of the town square, the murmur of traders and the occasional burst of laughter from the nearby performers filling the night air.

Player input: I draw my knife and lunge at Mitch.
FieldExpectedActualResult
has_two_sectionspasspassPASS
thoughts_before_narrativepasspassPASS
thoughts_is_first_personpasspassPASS

Generated Narrative
Your knife finds its mark as you lunge forward, slipping between Mitch’s belt and his torso. The blade bites deep, drawing a spray of dark blood that splatters the wooden floor and stains the hem of his shirt. Mitch lets out a guttural gasp, his hand clutching the wound as he staggers backward, the belt falling from his waist in a clatter of metal. The sudden burst of violence sends a wave of shock through the Copper Cup. Mara, the barkeep, slams a hand down on the counter, her eyes wide as she shouts for everyone to stay back. Brin lunges toward the back door, shouting a warning to the other patrons. Edda pulls a chair from under her table and hurls it toward the commotion, while Lysa scrambles to the bar, her face pale. Thom, already half-raised from his seat, drops his mug, the liquid spilling across the floor as he draws his own weapon, eyes fixed on you. The lanterns sway, casting flickering shadows over the chaos. Shouts, curses, and the clatter of chairs fill the air as the tavern erupts into a frenzy, the patrons scrambling for cover or to intervene. Mitch, bleeding but still standing, glares at you through his pain, his grip on his belt loosening as he tries to steady himself.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: I need to convey the immediate result of the successful attack: the knife hits Mitch, he’s wounded, blood, his belt maybe drops, and the tavern erupts into chaos with the other patrons reacting. I should mention the reactions of Brin, Edda, Lysa, and Thom, and Mara behind the counter, without dictating any further player actions. Keep it concise but vivid.

Narrative: Your knife finds its mark as you lunge forward, slipping between Mitch’s belt and his torso. The blade bites deep, drawing a spray of dark blood that splatters the wooden floor and stains the hem of his shirt. Mitch lets out a guttural gasp, his hand clutching the wound as he staggers backward, the belt falling from his waist in a clatter of metal.

The sudden burst of violence sends a wave of shock through the Copper Cup. Mara, the barkeep, slams a hand down on the counter, her eyes wide as she shouts for everyone to stay back. Brin lunges toward the back door, shouting a warning to the other patrons. Edda pulls a chair from under her table and hurls it toward the commotion, while Lysa scrambles to the bar, her face pale. Thom, already half-raised from his seat, drops his mug, the liquid spilling across the floor as he draws his own weapon, eyes fixed on you.

The lanterns sway, casting flickering shadows over the chaos. Shouts, curses, and the clatter of chairs fill the air as the tavern erupts into a frenzy, the patrons scrambling for cover or to intervene. Mitch, bleeding but still standing, glares at you through his pain, his grip on his belt loosening as he tries to steady himself.

FieldExpectedActualResult

Generated Narrative
The wooden door swings shut behind you, and the low‑beamed tavern swallows you in its warm, smoky haze. Lanterns sway gently above the crowded tables, casting amber pools of light over patrons nursing mugs of ale. The scent of hearty stew mingles with sea‑salt and woodsmoke, and the low murmur of conversation is punctuated by occasional laughter. Behind the polished bar, Mara—her dark hair tied back in a practical knot, a scarlet apron dusted with flour—glances up as you approach. She wipes a rag across the counter, eyes flicking from you to the bustling room and back again. “Back again, eh?” she says, a half‑smile tugging at the corner of her mouth. “You’re looking for that lock on the old lighthouse, right? Heard it’s set to open on a very specific night. The townsfolk say it’s tied to the tide—when the water’s at its highest during the full moon. That’s the best hint I’ve got. Anything else you need while you’re here?” Around the bar, Brin leans against a stool, polishing a dagger; Edda chats with Lysa over a plate of fried fish; Thom watches the door, his gaze lingering on the street beyond. The tavern hums with life, but Mara’s clue hangs in the air, waiting for you to act on it.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: The player has arrived at the Copper Cup and spoken with Mara. The mechanics already resolved that Mara gave a partial clue about the date lock. I need to convey that dialogue and briefly describe the tavern atmosphere, noting the other patrons present, without prompting further actions.

Narrative: The wooden door swings shut behind you, and the low‑beamed tavern swallows you in its warm, smoky haze. Lanterns sway gently above the crowded tables, casting amber pools of light over patrons nursing mugs of ale. The scent of hearty stew mingles with sea‑salt and woodsmoke, and the low murmur of conversation is punctuated by occasional laughter.

Behind the polished bar, Mara—her dark hair tied back in a practical knot, a scarlet apron dusted with flour—glances up as you approach. She wipes a rag across the counter, eyes flicking from you to the bustling room and back again.

“Back again, eh?” she says, a half‑smile tugging at the corner of her mouth. “You’re looking for that lock on the old lighthouse, right? Heard it’s set to open on a very specific night. The townsfolk say it’s tied to the tide—when the water’s at its highest during the full moon. That’s the best hint I’ve got. Anything else you need while you’re here?”

Around the bar, Brin leans against a stool, polishing a dagger; Edda chats with Lysa over a plate of fried fish; Thom watches the door, his gaze lingering on the street beyond. The tavern hums with life, but Mara’s clue hangs in the air, waiting for you to act on it.

Generated Narrative
You stride toward the direction of the Temple of the Tide, intent on confronting Cleric Serah, but the narrow lanes and bustling crowds of the square make it clear the sanctuary lies beyond your immediate reach. The stone-paved Town Square stretches only to the Copper Cup tavern, the East Alley, the Harbor Gate, and the nearby market stalls—none of which lead straight into the temple district. Lantern light flickers over the bronze fountain coin glinting at your feet, while traders haggle and dockhands shout about the day’s catch. The hum of conversation still circles the bloodstains and the mystery that haunts the town, but the temple itself remains a short distance away, across streets you’ll have to navigate first. You remain where you are, the weight of the unanswered questions pressing on you.
Raw LLM Output (last attempt)
Thoughts: The player wants to go to the temple, but movement is blocked because the temple isn’t adjacent to the Town Square. I need to tell them they can’t get there directly, describe the current surroundings, and hint that they’ll need to travel via other locations without suggesting specific actions.

Narrative: You stride toward the direction of the Temple of the Tide, intent on confronting Cleric Serah, but the narrow lanes and bustling crowds of the square make it clear the sanctuary lies beyond your immediate reach. The stone-paved Town Square stretches only to the Copper Cup tavern, the East Alley, the Harbor Gate, and the nearby market stalls—none of which lead straight into the temple district. Lantern light flickers over the bronze fountain coin glinting at your feet, while traders haggle and dockhands shout about the day’s catch. The hum of conversation still circles the bloodstains and the mystery that haunts the town, but the temple itself remains a short distance away, across streets you’ll have to navigate first. You remain where you are, the weight of the unanswered questions pressing on you.

Player input: I sprout wings and fly across the harbor to find Cleric Serah, then stab her before she can speak.
FieldExpectedActualResult
has_two_sectionspasspassPASS

narrative_no_bare_ipasspassPASS
